scenario: A C7-to-C8 migration team has a process with several Task Listeners: on-create (audit logging), on-assignment (notify assignee), on-completion (update downstream system). They wonder how to migrate each. Migration patterns for C7 Task Listeners in Camunda 8.
question: Migration patterns for C7 Task Listeners in Camunda 8.?

options.text: 7. 7, Camunda 8 includes native User Task Listeners implemented as <zeebe:taskListeners> extension elements. These provide direct counterparts to C7's on-create, on-assignment, and on-completion lifecycle hooks via five supported eventType values: creating, assigning, updating, completing, and canceling. Each listener is implemented by a job worker subscribed to the configured type string.

"/> elements. The 5 supported eventType values are creating, assigning, updating, completing, canceling (per :157). Migration map: C7 on-create → C8 eventType="creating"; C7 on-assignment → C8 eventType="assigning"; C7 on-completion → C8 eventType="completing". Each listener type is implemented by a job worker subscribing to the configured type string, using the same worker API as Service Tasks.

7+. A monolithic Service Task cannot differentiate between creating, assigning, and completing lifecycle moments as separate jobs. Per-listener patterns using <zeebe:taskListeners> with distinct eventType values provide the same lifecycle granularity that C7's Task Listeners offered.

The migration approach is to keep all Task Listeners running in a Camunda 7 environment indefinitely -- this defeats the goal of migrating to Camunda 8 and is not a viable long-term approach. Camunda 7 reaches end of life on October 31, 2025 for community edition and December 2027 for enterprise. Migration to Camunda 8's native User Task Listener mechanism (<zeebe:taskListeners>) provides the equivalent lifecycle hook functionality in a supported platform.

a.text: Incorrect. C7 Task Listeners were Java code / scripts attached as lifecycle hooks. C8 has native equivalent since 8.7 -- User Task Listeners via extension on a Camunda user task, with 5 lifecycle eventTypes per user-tasks.md:151-161. Migration map: C7 on-create → C8 eventType="creating": xml A job worker subscribed to type="audit-task-created" runs on task creation. Visible in Operate as a separate listener job. C7 on-assignment → C8 eventType="assigning": xml Fires when an assignee is set / changed. C7 on-completion → C8 eventType="completing": xml Fires when the user submits the task; the listener can validate / reject before the task is finalised. Additional C8 lifecycle hooks not in C7: eventType="updating" (variable update) and eventType="canceling" (task termination by parent). For complex listener logic, additional patterns: Dynamic assignee: FEEL in . Validation: eventType="completing" listener with reject capability (return non-zero to abort completion). Audit: combine creating + completing listeners for full lifecycle audit trail. Hoisted-to-flow alternative: if you prefer the listener logic to be visible in the BPMN diagram (rather than as a hook), use preceding / succeeding Service Tasks instead. The general principle: native task listeners preserve the C7 lifecycle-hook model in C8; choose hoisted Service Tasks only when diagram-level visibility is more important than separation of concerns.
